name: nodejs-backend-patterns
description: Build production-ready Node.js backend services with Express/Fastify, implementing middleware patterns, error handling, authentication, database integration, and API design best practices. Use when creating Node.js servers, REST APIs, GraphQL backends, or microservices architectures.
capabilities: Guide Express.js and Fastify project setup with security middleware
Implement layered architecture patterns (controllers, services, repositories)
Design authentication and authorization systems with JWT
Create database integration patterns for PostgreSQL and MongoDB
Implement error handling, logging, and rate limiting middleware
Provide code examples for caching, transactions, and dependency injection
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is an educational/documentation skill providing Node.js backend development guidance through markdown code examples. All static analysis findings are false positives - the detected patterns are instructional code samples in markdown format, not executable skill logic. No actual security risks detected.
valueStatement: Creating robust Node.js backends requires understanding complex architectural patterns and best practices. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for building scalable, secure backend services with modern frameworks.
limitations: Provides guidance and code examples, not executable automation
Does not replace the need for security review of production code
Examples may need adaptation for specific project requirements
Does not cover frontend integration or deployment strategies

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building web service APIs
useCases.title: API Development
useCases.description: Build REST or GraphQL APIs with proper middleware, validation, and error handling for production applications.

useCases.targetUser: Architects and senior developers designing distributed systems
useCases.title: Microservices Architecture
useCases.description: Design scalable microservices with dependency injection, proper logging, and inter-service communication patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Junior developers transitioning to backend development
useCases.title: Learning Modern Node.js
useCases.description: Learn industry-standard patterns for authentication, database access, caching, and error handling in Node.js applications.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Express Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a basic Express.js server with security middleware, request logging, and error handling. Include TypeScript types and proper configuration.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new Node.js API project

promptTemplates.title: Authentication System
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a JWT-based authentication system with login, registration, and token refresh endpoints. Include password hashing with bcrypt and proper error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding user authentication to an existing API

promptTemplates.title: Database Layer Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a repository pattern implementation for PostgreSQL with connection pooling, parameterized queries, and transaction support for an order management system.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building data access layer for an e-commerce application

promptTemplates.title: Complete Microservice
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a complete microservice architecture with Fastify, including dependency injection, health checks, metrics endpoints, and graceful shutdown handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a production-ready microservice for a distributed system

bestPractices: Always use TypeScript for type safety and better IDE support
Validate all user input with libraries like Zod before processing
Use environment variables for configuration and never commit secrets to version control
antiPatterns: Using wildcard CORS origins in production environments
Storing sensitive data like passwords or tokens in plain text
Blocking the event loop with synchronous operations or complex calculations

faq.question: Should I use Express or Fastify for my project?
faq.answer: Express has a larger ecosystem and is well-established. Fastify offers better performance with built-in schema validation. Choose Express for maximum compatibility or Fastify for high-throughput APIs.

faq.question: How do I handle database migrations in Node.js?
faq.answer: Use migration tools like Knex, Prisma, or typeorm. Define migrations as version-controlled files that can be applied or rolled back. Run migrations during deployment before starting the application.

faq.question: What is the recommended project structure for Node.js APIs?
faq.answer: Use layered architecture: controllers handle HTTP requests, services contain business logic, repositories manage data access. Keep configuration, middleware, types, and utilities in separate directories.

faq.question: How should I implement authentication in Node.js?
faq.answer: Use JWT for stateless authentication with short-lived access tokens and longer-lived refresh tokens. Hash passwords with bcrypt or argon2. Store secrets in environment variables and rotate them regularly.

faq.question: What logging approach should I use in production?
faq.answer: Use structured logging with Pino or Winston. Include correlation IDs for tracing requests across services. Log to stdout and aggregate logs with tools like ELK stack or cloud logging services.

faq.question: How do I ensure my Node.js API can handle high traffic?
faq.answer: Implement connection pooling for databases, add caching with Redis, use compression for responses, enable clustering to use all CPU cores, and implement proper rate limiting to prevent abuse.
